transcript:
I'm Prasanna, I lead protocols research at Anchorage. And today I'm going to be talking about VisualSign. It's a framework we wrote internally at Anchorage couple of years back to be able to support our business, our clients who are institutions holding some of the largest asset holders in the world. And we open sourced it so that we can provide tooling for the broader crypto ecosystem, including of course Solana. And we want to provide the best tooling, meet where the developers and the ecosystem is for everyone.

So let me talk a little bit about who Anchorage is. We are an industry-leading security infrastructure. What that means is that we have built our whole infrastructure to ensure that users always know what they are signing. Because we are working with large banks, large asset holders, foundations, and with large sums of money, there is a huge risk for every transaction. Over time we built support for custody, that's where we started out from, and we've increased our offering: trading, governance, settlement. And we also operate the only federally chartered crypto bank in the U.S. as well as other jurisdictions, and we have a self-custodial wallet called Porto.

As we started to integrate DeFi into our application and we started getting a lot of demand from institutions who are interested in using DeFi... what we started realizing is that DeFi has a visualization problem. What we have been doing is that as users, most of the experience is looking at these raw bytes... Just quick raise your hands who has signed a transaction with a wallet without looking at what exactly, without understanding what those raw bytes are? Yeah, I see a couple of hands... The main problem is we are actually training users to sign things they don't understand. It's fundamentally a problem.

And this is a problem with institutions. Institutions don't blind sign. If you have ever interacted with a company that has a legal document, how many lawyers have to go through a review process for moving any funds? In many cases, they may not be able to blind sign. We are acutely aware of these problems. And even outside of compliance, security-wise, if you get this wrong, it may be already too late... We are aware of other attempts... Solana Clear Sign... Wallets Native DeFi... But one of the other things that has been notable is usually it starts with disbelief that why doesn't this already exist? It seems like such an obvious problem. And after that, once I show some tooling, you actually hear, 'Oh, something like that already exists,' which means we as an industry are also duplicating a lot of effort.

So that's why we built VisualSign the way we built it. It's open source, it's reproducible, it operates inside a Trusted Execution Environment, so that you can actually run locally, you can build it locally, you don't have to use our infrastructure... Why are we building this for multi-chain? We believe that common standards that every chain, every builder that wants to build a new kind of chain can target will help us get to better design and better security outcomes.

So how did we do this? As I mentioned earlier, it runs in a Trusted Execution Environment. We partnered with Turnkey that operates this in a secure enclave in AWS Nitro Enclaves. We also open sourced how you can integrate this... The attestation gets verified by the signer just the same way our institutional infrastructure does... It's built on Rust... We are building this in a transparent, verifiable engineering practices... And for Solana, we built isolated programming interfaces... We are at the starting point right now. This doesn't cover everything... We would love to have simulations... We're starting with something high fidelity as much as possible.

One of the projects I wanted to highlight is Anagram. They have contributed a decoder for their Swig smart accounts... What this allows us to do is you can create a smart wallet with the safety of custody and the speed of internet capital markets. And if you are building some interesting applications that you want institutions to use... we'd love to talk to you. Looking forward to making transactions visible and transparent.

# Jupiter Unveils JupUSD and a New Era of Onchain Finance at Breakpoint
JupUSD anchors Jupiter’s push toward integrated, compliant, and institutional-grade onchain finance on Solana
![Yasmin Werner](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/cropped-cropped-CCIcon-32x32.png)By[Yasmin Werner](https://coincentral.com/author/yasmin/)December 12, 2025**3 Mins Read
![](https://coincentral.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/05/jupiter.jpg "Jupiter Unveils JupUSD and a New Era of Onchain Finance at Breakpoint")
## TLDR
* JupUSD becomes Jupiter’s core stablecoin across swaps, lending, perps, and strategies
* Jupiter Lend exits beta, boosting stablecoin efficiency with higher LTVs and lower penalties
* JupUSD shifts from standalone asset to infrastructure powering Solana-native DeFi
* KRW-pegged stablecoin signals Solana’s growing focus on regulated, institutional finance
* New data, verification, and trading tools support scalable, professional onchain markets
Jupiter announced a coordinated expansion of onchain finance capabilities at Breakpoint, centered on JupUSD and integrated infrastructure upgrades. The update combines stablecoins, lending, data, and trading into a single Solana-native stack. As a result, JupUSD anchors a broader push toward compliant, scalable, and professional onchain finance.
## JupUSD Expands Stablecoin Utility Across Jupiter
[Jupiter](https://coincentral.com/jupiter-targets-full-launch-of-prediction-market-platform-before-2026/)introduced JupUSD as a deeply integrated stablecoin designed to operate across its full product suite. The platform embedded JupUSD into swaps, perpetuals, lending, and automated strategies. Consequently, JupUSD supports consistent liquidity while sharing platform economics with active users.
JupUSD connects stable value with execution layers already processing billions in volume on Solana. Therefore, the design enables protocol-level coordination instead of isolated stablecoin usage. JupUSD also supports rewards during holding periods across multiple onchain features.
The rollout positions JupUSD as infrastructure rather than a standalone asset. Jupiter aligned issuance, routing, and incentives to strengthen onchain activity. As a result, JupUSD appears across web, mobile, and API surfaces as a default settlement asset.
Jupiter paired JupUSD with upgrades to lending and yield systems supporting stable demand. Jupiter Lend exited beta after rapid supply growth and released open source code. This environment allows JupUSD liquidity to move efficiently across borrowing, trading, and structured strategies.
Jupiter Lend uses tick-based liquidity built with Fluid for efficient liquidations. Consequently, the protocol offers higher loan ratios and lower penalties than comparable markets. These conditions

sources.title: Kraken to acquire Backed, accelerating xStocks expansion and unlocking global demand for tokenized equities

# [![Kraken Blog](https://i0.wp.com/blog.kraken.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/06/New-Lockups%402x.png?fit=214%2C64&ssl=1)](https://blog.kraken.com/)

December 2, 2025|[News](https://blog.kraken.com/category/news)
# Kraken to acquire Backed, accelerating xStocks expansion and unlocking global demand for tokenized equities
The Backed acquisition strengthens our tokenized asset leadership and marks a key milestone toward making global capital markets open, accessible and borderless.
### Share this:
* [Click to share on X (Opens in new window)X](https://blog.kraken.com/news/backed-acquisition?share=twitter)
* [Click to share on Facebook (Opens in new window)Facebook](https://blog.kraken.com/news/backed-acquisition?share=facebook)
* ![](https://i0.wp.com/blog.kraken.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/xStocks-Backed-Annoucement-Blog.png?fit=3070%2C1400&ssl=1)
We are excited to announce we have agreed to acquire[Backed Finance AG](https://backed.fi/), the company driving the issuance of[xStocks](https://xstocks.com/), the leading industry standard for tokenized equities. The acquisition will accelerate the global adoption of xStocks; unifying issuance, trading and settlement to power open capital markets infrastructure for everyone.
xStocks has seen exceptional adoption since its debut earlier in 2025, surpassing $10 billion in combined exchange and onchain trading volume within six months. This acquisition gives Backed access to the capital and resources needed to scale faster and capitalize on surging adoption and growing institutional demand for tokenized equities.
Bringing Backed fully into our ecosystem also allows greater alignment on strategy and investment priorities, unlocking the full economic potential of xStocks.
Together, Kraken and Backed will accelerate xStocks’ expansion, bringing tokenized equities to new markets and extending their reach into everyday financial experiences. The acquisition lays the groundwork for future innovation in tokenized assets beyond equities, advancing our leadership in building open, interoperable financial infrastructure.
xStocks currently offers exposure to more than 60 tokenized equities and ETFs backed 1:1 by the underlying asset. The tokens are live on Solana and Ethereum, with integrations on TON, Tron, Mantle and BNB Chain coming soon.
Investors can trade 24 hours a day, five days a week on Kraken, or directly onchain 24/7, creating a seamless, continuous bridge between traditional and crypto markets. xStocks can be self-custodied and were designed for multi-chain composability, keeping the infrastructure open and usable across the broader ecosystem.
Through the xStocks Alliance, Kraken and Backed have built a thriving ecosystem of partners spanning blockchain foundations, trading venues, and consumer applications. With this acquisition, we plan to further expand this ecosystem, enhancing interoperability and liquidity for tokenized equities across the industry.
**Arjun Sethi, Kraken Co-CEO**: “Integrating Backed into Kraken strengthens the core architecture required for open and programmable capital markets. Unifying issuance, trading and settlement under one framework ensures the infrastructure for tokenized assets remains transparent, reliable and globally accessible. This is foundational work for the next era of market structure. This is bigger than giving people exposure to U.S. equities, it’s about redefining what it means to own assets in the digital era.”
Backed’s team and infrastructure will become part of Kraken, strengthening issuance and compliance capabilities while expanding the reach and utility of tokenized assets beyond the crypto ecosystem

tags.impact_reason: VisualSign is a useful open-source tool from Anchorage that could improve security for Solana developers, but its impact will depend on adoption.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a transparent, crystalline bank vault door as the Semantic Anchor. This is because the story is about Anchorage Digital, a federally chartered crypto bank, releasing a tool to make opaque transactions ('blind signing') visible and secure. The vault represents the institutional, high-security context, while its transparency directly visualizes the core function of 'VisualSign'—making the inner workings of a transaction clear and understandable. For the portrait, I included the diegetic text 'VERIFIED' holographically projected inside the mechanism. This single, high-impact word represents the successful outcome of the visualization process: a transaction that is understood, confirmed, and secure, which is the entire goal for the institutional clients mentioned.
visual_idea.description: A macro, 1:1 view focusing on a section of the transparent vault door's mechanism. A massive, polished chrome locking bolt is seen sliding perfectly into its crystalline glass housing. The shot emphasizes the extreme detail of the materials—the flawless clarity of the glass and the mirror-like finish of the metal. Light refracts through the complex geometry, creating sharp caustics and specular highlights, conveying a feeling of ultimate precision and security.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A macro, 1:1 view, perfectly symmetrical and centrally focused, filling the entire square frame with intricate texture. The subject is a section of a transparent vault door's mechanism. A massive, gleaming polished chrome locking bolt, rendered with a mirror-like finish and hyper-realistic metallic reflections, is seen sliding perfectly into its crystalline glass housing. The shot emphasizes the extreme detail of the materials; the flawless, optically pure clarity of the glass and the precise, mirror-like finish of the chrome are paramount. Cool, crisp institutional lighting highlights the complex geometry. Light intensely refracts and reflects through the crystalline glass and polished chrome, creating razor-sharp caustics and brilliant specular highlights. Subtle, ethereal glows emanate from within the glass, hinting at a holographic interface, conveying a feeling of ultimate precision and security. The image is rendered with an emphasis on hyperrealism and photoreal detail, showcasing intricate architectural elements and rich textures. 8k, octane render, raytracing, physically based rendering (PBR), hyper-detailed, high dynamic range, cinematic contrast.

editorial.headline: Anchorage Digital Open Sources 'VisualSign' to End Blind Signing for Institutions
editorial.standfirst: The federally chartered crypto bank releases its internal visualization framework to standardize transaction safety. The move aims to eliminate 'blind signing' risks that currently block large-scale institutional DeFi adoption.
key_takeaways: Anchorage Digital has open-sourced "VisualSign," a framework designed to convert raw transaction bytecode into human-readable formats for compliance-heavy institutions.
The system runs inside a Trusted Execution Environment (TEE) via AWS Nitro Enclaves (partnering with Turnkey), ensuring that transaction previews are cryptographically verifiable and tamper-proof.
Solana developer Anagram has already contributed a decoder for its Swig smart wallet, demonstrating immediate utility for programmable accounts on the network.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
For years, the crypto industry has normalized a dangerous practice: "blind signing." Users, from retail traders to fund managers, routinely approve transactions based on raw hexadecimal bytecode they cannot read. At Breakpoint 2025, **Anchorage Digital**—the only federally chartered crypto bank in the U.S.—declared this practice incompatible with institutional capital. Prasanna Gautam, Lead of Protocols Research, unveiled **VisualSign**, an open-source framework designed to render raw on-chain data into clear, verifiable English before a signature is ever applied.

The stakes for Anchorage’s clients—which include sovereign wealth funds and large banks—are existential. A single blind signature on a malicious contract could drain a treasury. **VisualSign** addresses this by operating within a **Trusted Execution Environment (TEE)**, specifically leveraging **AWS Nitro Enclaves** through a partnership with **Turnkey**. This architecture ensures that the visualization logic runs in a secure, isolated container that cannot be tampered with by compromised front-ends or malware. It provides a "What You See Is What You Sign" guarantee that compliance departments demand.

Crucially, Anchorage is positioning VisualSign not as a proprietary product, but as a public good to standardize security across the ecosystem. By open-sourcing the code, they are inviting protocol developers to write their own "decoders." **Anagram**, a Solana-based developer team, has already integrated the framework for their **Swig** smart accounts. This allows complex, programmable wallet interactions to be parsed instantly into human-readable terms, bridging the gap between advanced Solana programmability and institutional safety requirements.

This release signals a maturity shift for Solana's DeFi landscape. As long as transaction data remains opaque, institutional participation is capped by risk management policies. By decoupling transaction visualization from specific wallet providers and making it a reproducible standard, Anchorage is laying the technical groundwork for billions in regulated capital to enter on-chain markets with confidence.
